comment_text.sn1.33:10.1: Jantu is literally, “one who is born”, and originally had the sense of “child” (Rig Veda 9.67.13) and hence “kin”. In the Suttas it occasionally retains that meaning (SN 11.11:2.1), but normally just means a “person”. It is exclusively reserved for poetry, so I use the elevated “personage”.
comment_text.sn1.33:10.3: The Vetaraṇī is described at Snp 3.10:26.1. It features in the later literature of the Brahmins (eg. Bhāgavata Purāṇa 5.26.22, Skanda Purāṇa 6.1.226.32) and Jains (Sūtrakṛtāṅga 1.5.1.8).
comment_text.sn1.33:16.2: Both PTS and BJT have the singular karoti here.
comment_text.sn1.33:19.1: Read saddhā rather than variant addhā (also at Ja 424:8. and its commentary). I think here he is affirming the good points made by the deities before making his own contribution.
comment_text.sn1.33:19.4: The lesson of this verse is that study of “passages of teaching” (dhammapada) stimulates wisdom leading to liberation.

root_text.sn1.33:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā satullapakāyikā devatāyo abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.sn1.33:1.3: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho ekā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.sn1.33:3.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.sn1.33:6.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.sn1.33:8.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.sn1.33:11.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.sn1.33:12.5: Api ca viceyya dānampi sāhu dānaṁ.
root_text.sn1.33:14.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.sn1.33:16.3: Bhīruṁ pasaṁsanti na hi tattha sūraṁ,
root_text.sn1.33:16.4: Bhayā hi santo na karonti pāpan”ti.
root_text.sn1.33:17.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.33:18.1: “Sabbāsaṁ vo subhāsitaṁ pariyāyena, api ca mamapi suṇātha—
root_text.sn1.33:19.3: Pubbe ca hi pubbatare ca santo,

translation_text.sn1.33:0.2: 4. The Host of a Hundred Felicities
translation_text.sn1.33:1.2: Then, late at night, several glorious deities of the host of a hundred felicities, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side.
translation_text.sn1.33:1.3: Standing to one side, one deity expressed this heartfelt sentiment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.33:3.1: Then another deity expressed this heartfelt sentiment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.33:4.2: Even when one has little, giving is good.
translation_text.sn1.33:5.1: Some who have little are happy to provide,
translation_text.sn1.33:5.2: while some who have much don’t wish to give.
translation_text.sn1.33:6.1: Then another deity expressed this heartfelt sentiment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.33:7.2: Even when one has little, giving is good.
translation_text.sn1.33:7.3: And it’s also good to give out of faith.
translation_text.sn1.33:7.4: Giving and warfare are similar, they say,
translation_text.sn1.33:7.5: for even a few of the good may conquer the many.
translation_text.sn1.33:7.6: If a faithful person gives even a little,
translation_text.sn1.33:7.7: it still brings them happiness in the hereafter.”
translation_text.sn1.33:8.1: Then another deity expressed this heartfelt sentiment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.33:9.2: Even when one has little, giving is good.
translation_text.sn1.33:9.3: And it’s also good to give out of faith.
translation_text.sn1.33:9.4: And it’s also good to give legitimate wealth.
translation_text.sn1.33:10.1: A personage who gives legitimate wealth,
translation_text.sn1.33:10.2: earned by his efforts and initiative,
translation_text.sn1.33:10.3: has passed over Yama’s Vetaraṇī River;
translation_text.sn1.33:10.4: that mortal arrives at celestial fields.”
translation_text.sn1.33:11.1: Then another deity expressed this heartfelt sentiment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.33:12.2: Even when one has little, giving is good.
translation_text.sn1.33:12.3: And it’s also good to give out of faith.
translation_text.sn1.33:12.4: And it’s also good to give legitimate wealth.
translation_text.sn1.33:12.5: And it’s also good to give intelligently.
translation_text.sn1.33:13.1: The Holy One praises giving intelligently
translation_text.sn1.33:13.2: to those worthy of offerings here  <j>in the world of the living.
translation_text.sn1.33:13.3: What’s given to these is very fruitful,
translation_text.sn1.33:13.4: like seeds sown in a fertile field.”
translation_text.sn1.33:14.1: Then another deity expressed this heartfelt sentiment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.33:15.2: Even when one has little, giving is good.
translation_text.sn1.33:15.3: And it’s also good to give out of faith.
translation_text.sn1.33:15.4: And it’s also good to give legitimate wealth.
translation_text.sn1.33:15.5: And it’s also good to give intelligently.
translation_text.sn1.33:15.6: And it’s also good to be restrained  <j>when it comes to living creatures.
translation_text.sn1.33:16.1: One who lives without harming any living being
translation_text.sn1.33:16.2: does no bad because of others’ blame.
translation_text.sn1.33:16.3: Were that the case they praise the coward, not the brave;
translation_text.sn1.33:16.4: for the virtuous do no bad out of fear.”
translation_text.sn1.33:17.1: Then another deity said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn1.33:18.1: “You’ve all spoken well in your own way. However, listen to me also:
translation_text.sn1.33:19.1: A gift of faith is praised in many ways,
translation_text.sn1.33:19.2: but a passage of teaching is better than giving,
translation_text.sn1.33:19.3: for the virtuous, in days old and older still,
